key_insight: A single factorized form, \u03c1 = FF\u2020, can encode many physically valid quantum-state models while unifying low-rank, tensor-network, and neural priors for tomography.
engineering_relevance: This gives a scalable route to quantum state reconstruction that reduces parameter/sample burden and can be adapted to different hardware, data regimes, and model assumptions.
method: Represent the density matrix as FF\u2020 with F chosen from a structured model class.
Formulate QST as optimization over the factor space using least-squares or maximum-likelihood objectives.
Use projected gradient descent or a geometry-aware power method for efficient reconstruction; the latter is step-size-free.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a unified statistical analysis of least-squares sample complexity and proposes optimization algorithms validated across a broad class of structured quantum states.
watch_item: Performance depends on choosing an appropriate factor structure; mismatched priors or nonconvex optimization can make reconstruction sensitive to initialization and constraint handling.
paper_title: Structured Factorization Approaches for Quantum State Tomography
